question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockFileTransformer.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: transform()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a BlockFile where hasRecordFile() returns true to bypass the full transformation pipeline in transform(), causing the embedded RecordFile to be passed directly to streamFileNotifier.verified() without re-verifying token transfer lists, leading to incorrect fund balances being persisted?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockFileTransformer.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: transform()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a BlockFile with a manipulated hapiProtoVersion (major=0, minor=0, patch=0) so that the resulting RecordFile carries a zeroed hapiVersion, causing downstream processors to misinterpret transaction semantics and misroute token transfer amounts, leading to direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockFileTransformer.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: transform()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a BlockFile with a crafted softwareVersion (getMajor/getMinor/getPatch returning attacker-controlled values) so that the RecordFile.softwareVersionMajor/Minor/Patch fields are set to values that trigger a version-specific code path in downstream consumers, causing incorrect token balance calculations and direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockFileTransformer.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: transform()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a BlockFile where fileHash is always set to StringUtils.EMPTY in the resulting RecordFile, bypassing any downstream hash integrity check that would otherwise detect tampered transfer lists, leading to undetected fund manipulation?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockFileTransformer.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: transform()] Can an unprivileged external user provide a BlockFile with a manipulated getCount() value that does not match the actual number of items, causing downstream balance reconciliation logic to skip or double-count transactions and produce incorrect fund balances?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockFileTransformer.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: transform()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a BlockFile with a crafted getDigestAlgorithm() value that is accepted without validation in transform(), causing the RecordFile to carry an unsupported or weak digest algorithm that allows hash collisions and tampered transfer data to pass verification?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockFileTransformer.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: transform()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a BlockFile where getHash() returns an attacker-controlled value that is blindly copied to RecordFile.hash in transform(), bypassing the hash chain integrity check and allowing a block with manipulated token transfers to be accepted as valid?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockFileTransformer.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: transform()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a BlockFile with a crafted getPreviousHash() that does not match the actual previous block hash, and if transform() copies it without re-validation to RecordFile.previousHash, cause the hash chain to be silently broken, allowing injected blocks with fraudulent transfers to be persisted?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockFileTransformer.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: transform()] Can an unprivileged external user provide a BlockFile with a manipulated getIndex() (block number) that is copied verbatim to RecordFile.index in transform(), causing the block to be stored at an incorrect position in the chain and allowing a replay of a previously-processed block with different transfer amounts?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockFileTransformer.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: transform()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a BlockFile with getConsensusStart() and getConsensusEnd() timestamps that are out of order or in the future, causing transform() to produce a RecordFile with invalid consensus window that bypasses time-based deduplication checks and allows double-spending of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockFileTransformer.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getRecordItems()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a BlockFile with an empty items list so that getRecordItems() returns Collections.emptyList() without error, causing a block that should contain fund-transferring transactions to be silently treated as empty and those transfers to be permanently lost?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockFileTransformer.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getRecordItems()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a BlockFile whose items list contains a null BlockTransaction entry, causing a NullPointerException during the reverse-order loop in getRecordItems() that aborts processing of the entire block and causes all fund transfers in that block to be lost?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockFileTransformer.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getRecordItems()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a BlockFile with a single-element items list containing a crafted BlockTransaction, exploiting the reverse-order processing logic in getRecordItems() so that the transactionIndex is set to 0 for a transaction that should have a non-zero index, causing incorrect parent-child linking and misattribution of token transfers?
